heading: Estimates based on tax reporting system
label: 1610 Estimates based on tax reporting system
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsecs. (c) and (d)(2), (3), was in the original “this Act” meaning , , , known as the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995. (a). , in introductory provisions, substituted “A person, other than a lobbying firm,” for “A registrant”.Pub. L. 105–166, § 4(a)(1)Subsec. (a)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “in lieu of using the definition of ‘lobbying activities’ in , consider as lobbying activities only those activities that are influencing legislation as defined in .”Pub. L. 105–166, § 4(a)(2)section 1602(7) of this titlesection 4911(d) of title 26Subsec. (b). , in introductory provisions, substituted “A person, other than a lobbying firm, who is required to account and does account for lobbying expenditures pursuant to” for “A registrant that is subject to”.Pub. L. 105–166, § 4(b)(1)Subsec. (b)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “in lieu of using the definition of ‘lobbying activities’ in , consider as lobbying activities only those activities, the costs of which are not deductible pursuant to .”Pub. children.heading: Entities covered bysection 6033(b) of title 26
children.label: a Entities covered bysection 6033(b) of title 26

children.text: make a good faith estimate (by category of dollar value) of applicable amounts that would be required to be disclosed under such section for the appropriate quarterly period to meet the requirements of sections 1603(a)(3) and 1604(b)(4) of this title; and

children.label: A
children.text: lobbying contacts with covered legislative branch officials (as defined in ) and lobbying activities in support of such contacts; andsection 1602(4) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: lobbying of Federal executive branch officials to the extent that such activities are influencing legislation as defined in .section 4911(d) of title 26

children.heading: Entities covered bysection 162(e) of title 26
children.label: b Entities covered bysection 162(e) of title 26

children.text: make a good faith estimate (by category of dollar value) of applicable amounts that would not be deductible pursuant to such section for the appropriate quarterly period to meet the requirements of sections 1603(a)(3) and 1604(b)(4) of this title; and

children.label: A
children.text: lobbying contacts with covered legislative branch officials (as defined in ) and lobbying activities in support of such contacts; andsection 1602(4) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: lobbying of Federal executive branch officials to the extent that amounts paid or costs incurred in connection with such activities are not deductible pursuant to .section 162(e) of title 26
inform the Secretary of the Senate and the Clerk of the House of Representatives that the registrant has elected to make its estimates under such procedures; and
make all such estimates, in a given calendar year, under such procedures.

the differences between the definition of “lobbying activities” in and the definitions of “lobbying expenditures”, “influencing legislation”, and related terms in sections 162(e) and 4911 of title 26, as each are implemented by regulations;section 1602(7) of this title
the impact that any such differences may have on filing and reporting under this chapter pursuant to this subsection; and
any changes to this chapter or to the appropriate sections of title 26 that the Comptroller General may recommend to harmonize the definitions.
